source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - West Delta 90 B Platform Loss of Well Control

context.region: Gulf of America, West Delta Block 90

actions_taken: Full platform evacuation to E platform
All personnel mustered and evacuated via MV GO Discovery
Two personnel returned to B platform to de-energize equipment and close crown valve
Closure of lower master valve and crown valve to shut in well
Installation of secondary containment measures including acyclic berm at wellhead
P&A operations resumed on April 24, 2025 with no further hydrocarbon detection
event.summary: On April 21, 2025, at 19:30 CDT, the WD 90 B platform experienced a loss of well control during Plug and Abandonment operations on well B020. An uncontrolled release of approximately 20 barrels of well fluids occurred due to a leak at the starter head spool. The incident was triggered when the P&A crew pumped fluid down the tubing to assist pressure equalization across a downhole plug. Oil was observed leaking from between the starter head flange and the surface casing annulus. After approximately 5-10 minutes, the fluid flow reduced and stopped, but uncontrolled gas was then observed, prompting full platform evacuation. All 22 personnel were safely evacuated. The well was shut in by closing the crown valve and cutting the wireline. No injuries were reported.
recommendations: Inclusion of complete and accurate wellbore history in procedures
Implementation of structured hazard assessment process
Earlier implementation of engineering controls such as rubberized sealant at spooling head
Systematic contingency planning and information sharing

hazards.name: Defective or degraded well completion components

threats.name: Breach in starter head spool during P&A operations
Tubing string failure enabling hydraulic communication
Failure to identify and mitigate known well integrity issues

consequences.name: Uncontrolled release of well fluids to environment

controls.name: Slickline Lubricator pressure control
controls.barrier_role: Isolate and control pressure in tubing during wireline operations
controls.lod_basis: Primary well control equipment for slickline operations

supporting_text: The well control equipment (SLK Lubricator) only controls pressure in the tubing, but is not effective if a leak in the casing strings is present.

After approximately 15 minutes of waiting, due to the small equalization ports on the plug, the decision was made by the CES supervisor and SLK operator to pump fluid down the tubing to help with the equalization process.

controls.name: Surface-Controlled Subsurface Safety Valve (SCSSV)
controls.barrier_role: Provide subsurface isolation and well control

supporting_text: The CES Crew then locked open the Surface-Controlled Subsurface Safety Valve (SCSSV) and ran SLK in the well
The BPV was not found at 506' (MD).

In the Apache procedure it directed the crew to remove the back pressure valve, an action that was not applicable, as the valve was no longer present in the well having been extracted during the well work previously mentioned.

controls.name: Pre-job safety meeting and hazard assessment
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and establish work-stop authority before operations
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory requirement and operational safety procedure

supporting_text: No documentation could be provided showing the safety meeting agenda and attendees for the crew on shift during the event.

Once the starter head spool was found to be leaking, there was no stopping point by the crew to discuss what potential hazards may exists and how to prevent/mitigate this risk before continuing with the abandon procedure.

controls.name: Wellbore history documentation and procedure reconciliation
controls.barrier_role: Ensure accurate well information is incorporated into operational procedures

supporting_text: A breakdown in communication between successive work crews operating on the platform contributed to critical procedural oversights.
The operational procedure in use was deficient as it failed to account for prior well activity conducted on 06-Apr-2019 and 17-Aug-2021.

Review of historical well reports from these dates indicates the spooling head had a documented history of leakage, and that the DS plug had been installed in response to operators detecting gas emissions at the surface-specifically from the drive pip beneath the tree. This prior information was not integrated into the current operational planning.

controls.name: Crew knowledge and experience verification
controls.barrier_role: Ensure crew has detailed knowledge of specific well history and contingencies

supporting_text: Although the CES Crew possessed extensive cumulative experience in Plug and Abandonment operations, the lack of detailed knowledge regarding the specific well history limited their ability to anticipate and mitigate risk.
The CES Crew was not aware of why the leak was occurring or well control contingencies for this event.

controls.name: Immediate response to leak detection
controls.barrier_role: Stop operations and implement containment when leak is detected

supporting_text: Upon observation of this leak, the CES supervisor called an all stop over the radio, and the pump operator shut down the pump.
After an estimated 5 to 10 minutes the fluid flowing from the well reduced and stopped.

controls.name: Platform evacuation and muster procedures
controls.barrier_role: Remove personnel from hazard area and account for all staff

supporting_text: Once the flow of fluid ceased, uncontrolled gas was observed coming from the starter head prompting a full site evacuation. All personnel from the 'B' platform were evacuated to the 'E' platform, where a full muster was announced.
The muster alarm was sounded, and the 22 POB moved across the gangway from the 'B' structure to the 'E' structure

controls.name: Well shut-in by closing crown valve and cutting wireline
controls.barrier_role: Isolate well and stop hydrocarbon flow

supporting_text: Two members of the team crossed back to the 'B' structure, cut the wire, closed the lower master valve, and then closed the crown valve.
Closure of the two valves slowed the flow 'substantially' but not completely.

controls.name: Secondary containment measures (acyclic berm)
controls.barrier_role: Contain and prevent environmental release of spilled fluids

supporting_text: The CES crew had implemented secondary containment measures, including the installation of a purpose-built acyclic berm secured to the wellhead, to mitigate potential future discharges.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
